title: Commentary on Members: As of March 31, 2026
content:
The credit union's membership count remained flat quarter over quarter as of March 31, 2026.

Members per full-time employees trended slightly upward from year-end 2025 through the first quarter of 2026. This indicates that staffing levels are aligned with service demand for current members.

Shares per member declined during this period. Existing members hold a smaller average deposit balance, which affects the funding base per member even if total membership is unchanged; this decline occurred while the number of current members remained stable quarter over quarter.

The credit union maintained flat headcount and rising efficiency in Q1 2026 despite lower deposits-per-member. Continued focus on engagement and retention will support deposit growth tied to the specific consequence identified above.
